content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of length NNN. You can perform the following operation on it any number of times (possibly, zero):


Choose any index iii (1≤i≤N1\leq i \leq N1≤i≤N) and change AiA_iAi​ to 000.
Your goal is to maximize the value of the maximum subarray sum of AAA.

Find the **minimum number of operations** needed to achieve this.

Note that an **empty subarray** has a sum of 000.

Formally,


For any pair of integers (L,R)(L, R)(L,R), let s(L,R)s(L, R)s(L,R) denote the subarray sum function, where:

If 1≤L≤R≤N1 \leq L \leq R \leq N1≤L≤R≤N, then s(L,R)=AL+AL+1+…+ARs(L, R) = A_L + A_{L+1} + \ldots + A_Rs(L,R)=AL​+AL+1​+…+AR​
Otherwise, s(L,R)=0s(L, R) = 0s(L,R)=0.


For the array AAA, define f(A):=max⁡(s(L,R))f(A) := \max (s(L, R))f(A):=max(s(L,R)) across all pairs (L,R)(L, R)(L,R) of integers.

This is the maximum subarray sum of array AAA.
Your goal is to make f(A)f(A)f(A) as large as possible by performing the given operations on the array AAA.

Find the minimum number of operations needed to achieve this goal.

Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains NNN — the number of elements in AAA.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​, denoting the array AAA.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the minimum number of operations required to achieve the maximum possible subarray sum.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 4⋅1054 \cdot 10^54⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The current maximum subarray sum is 000, obtained by taking the empty subarray.
It can be shown that no matter how we perform operations, this will not change.
So, no operations need to be done, and the minimum number of operations required is 000.
Test case 222: Change A2A_2A2​ to 000 using the operation, making the array [1,0,1][1, 0, 1][1,0,1].
This will make the maximum subarray sum 222, which is the maximum possible.
Using 111 operation this way is optimal.
Test case 333: Change A3A_3A3​ and A4A_4A4​ to 000 using the operation, turning the array into [1,2,0,0,4][1, 2, 0, 0, 4][1,2,0,0,4].
This will make the maximum subarray sum 777, which is the maximum possible.
We needed 222 operations to do this.
